visa_types.name: Resident Permit
visa_types.overview:
Type D Visa
Please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/have-applied/family-immigration/#link-1228) for information and procedures regarding a type D visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Type D Visa
Please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/have-applied/family-immigration/#link-1228) for information and procedures regarding a type D visa.

## Residence Permits
For more details on complete overview of different visas and residence permits please visit the Norwegian Embassy´s website (https://www.norway.no/) or the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/) .

## Vfs service charge

[Table:Resident Permit]

There is service charge and courier fee of 100 euro/65500 FCFA levied per application over and above the visa fees. (Service charge is inclusive of all taxes) The fee mentioned is per applicant.
Courier resubmission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay (93 Euro/60900 FCFA) to cover the Resubmission Courier”.
There is no exemption for VFS Global Service charge and mandatory courier fee.
The service charge and mandatory courier fee are to be paid by at VFS Global Centre.
The above fees are non-refundable and non transferable.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa
visa_types.overview:
Type C Visa
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Norway Visa Application
                                                            Centre in Cameroon.
If you plan to visit more than one Schengen country in the course of one trip, or if you plan several separate trips within a period of two months, you must submit your application to the embassy
                                                            or consulate of the Member State that is your main destination based on the length of your stay, or the purpose of stay.
If no main destination can be determined, the Member State whose external border the applicant intends to cross first in order to enter the territory of the Member States.
Note that It is a short term period visa (type "C") with a Maximum length of stay of 90 days.
Please click below links to view the list of documents you need to submit at the Visa Application Centre.
- Tourist Checklist and Questionnaire
- Business Checklist and Questionnaire
- Visit Family and Friends Checklist and Questionnaire
- Sport/Culture/Football Checklist and Questionnaire
Please click here if you are travelling with, or visiting, a family member who is an EU/EEA national, these documents are required when applying for a visa,
                                                            also include the invitation from sponsors. when the purpose is visit to family or friends
visa_types.full_text:
## Type C Visa
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before applying at the Norway Visa Application
                                                            Centre in Cameroon.

                                                            or consulate of the Member State that is your main destination based on the length of your stay, or the purpose of stay.
If no main destination can be determined, the Member State whose external border the applicant intends to cross first in order to enter the territory of the Member States.
Note that It is a short term period visa (type "C") with a Maximum length of stay of 90 days.
